structured.title: Vin And Elend Confront Politics
structured.overview: Vin and Elend discuss the corruption in Luthadel, the burden of politics, and the tension between personal trust and public power. Elend defends his concern for the people and admits he was foolish for avoiding Vin, while Vin pushes back that politics changes everyone, especially those at the top. The exchange also touches on past abuse and guilt, with Elend revealing empathy and remorse, and Vin ultimately believing he is not a monster.
